items.title: In 2018-19 (the year before Leon Rose took over), the Knicks didn't win thier 14th regular-season game until March 17th(!)
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
In 2018-19 (the year before Leon Rose took over), the Knicks didn't win thier 14th regular-season game until March 17th(!) 

They had lost 56 of their first 69 games that season

Tonight, the Knicks are attempting to win thier 14th PLAYOFF GAME IN A ROW (they haven't lost a single game in 7 weeks)

items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
Karl-Anthony Towns has posted a plus/minus of +3 or higher in every one of the Knicks' 16 playoff games. 

That's the longest such streak in NBA postseason history (ahead of Steph, Draymond and LeBron).

items.title: In the Spurs' wins this postseason, Victor Wembanyama is averaging:
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
In the Spurs' wins this postseason, Victor Wembanyama is averaging:
28.5 points
12.6 rebounds
while shooting
56% from the floor and 
45% from downtown 

In the Spurs' losses this postseason, Wemby is averaging:
17. 8 points
8.9 rebounds
while shooting:
39% from the floor and 
21% from downtown

items.title: June 8, 1982: Lakers rookie Kurt Rambis dunks on Darryl Dawkins of the Philadelphia 76ers in Game 6 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 8, 1982: Lakers rookie Kurt Rambis dunks on Darryl Dawkins of the Philadelphia 76ers in Game 6 of the NBA Finals.

Los Angeles won, 114-104, and clinched the NBA World Championship. Jamaal Wilkes scored 27 PTS and Magic Johnson had 13 PTS/13/REB/13 AST/4 STL for the Lakers.

items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
Incredibly, this is a photo taken just now (5:30 pm EST) of the MSG entrance above Penn Station. 

Eerily quiet. 

Strangely empty.

Hours before the biggest Knicks game in a generation, the epicenter of New York basketball looks like a ghost town.

items.title: Bonus Hollander POTD Anthony Bonner. St Louis PF was hustler drafted 23rd by SAC in 1990. Good energy/defense guy played for SAC thru 1993. NY signed him in 93 & NY 29-9 when he started. Lack of shooting held him back and he was out of NBA by 1996. Played in Europe thru 2006.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Bonus Hollander POTD Anthony Bonner. St Louis PF was hustler drafted 23rd by SAC in 1990. Good energy/defense guy played for SAC thru 1993. NY signed him in 93 & NY 29-9 when he started. Lack of shooting held him back and he was out of NBA by 1996. Played in Europe thru 2006.

items.title: Wrote about this last week but the Sky lead this category mostly because they chose *in free agency* to start the season with 1/4 of their roster on the IR.
items.author: Julia Poe
items.body_excerpt:
Wrote about this last week but the Sky lead this category mostly because they chose *in free agency* to start the season with 1/4 of their roster on the IR. 

A hindrance, but certainly not a surprise.

items.title: New @WNBAonCLNS podcast with @jennhatfield1 !
items.author: Noa Dalzell
items.body_excerpt:
New @WNBAonCLNS podcast with @jennhatfield1 !

Talked Sydney Johnson’s ejection, Sonia Citron and Kiki Iriafen’s sophomore seasons, Lauren Betts rookie season, the point guard conundrum, and much more Washington Mystics stuff: 
youtu.be/9iXVMvO75gA?...

items.title: "This is your last chance. After this, there is no turning back. You take the blue pill — the story ends, you wake up in your bed and believe whatever you want to believe. You take the red pill — Giannis is traded."
items.author: J.E. Skeets
items.body_excerpt: "This is your last chance. After this, there is no turning back. You take the blue pill — the story ends, you wake up in your bed and believe whatever you want to believe. You take the red pill — Giannis is traded."
